root_text.an10.84:1.1: Tatra kho āyasmā mahāmoggallāno bhikkhū āmantesi:
root_text.an10.84:1.3: “Āvuso”ti kho te bhikkhū āyasmato mahāmoggallānassa paccassosuṁ.
root_text.an10.84:2.2: ‘khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyāti pajānāmī’ti.
root_text.an10.84:2.3: Tamenaṁ tathāgato vā tathāgatasāvako vā jhāyī samāpattikusalo paracittakusalo paracittapariyāyakusalo samanuyuñjati samanuggāhati samanubhāsati.
root_text.an10.84:2.4: So tathāgatena vā tathāgatasāvakena vā jhāyinā samāpattikusalena paracittakusalena paracittapariyāyakusalena samanuyuñjiyamāno samanuggāhiyamāno samanubhāsiyamāno irīṇaṁ āpajjati vicinaṁ āpajjati anayaṁ āpajjati byasanaṁ āpajjati anayabyasanaṁ āpajjati.
root_text.an10.84:3.1: Tamenaṁ tathāgato vā tathāgatasāvako vā jhāyī samāpattikusalo paracittakusalo paracittapariyāyakusalo evaṁ cetasā ceto paricca manasi karoti:
root_text.an10.84:3.3: khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyāti pajānāmī’ti?
root_text.an10.84:4.1: Tamenaṁ tathāgato vā tathāgatasāvako vā jhāyī samāpattikusalo paracittakusalo paracittapariyāyakusalo evaṁ cetasā ceto paricca pajānāti:
root_text.an10.84:5.3: Kodhapariyuṭṭhānaṁ kho pana tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye parihānametaṁ.
root_text.an10.84:6.3: Upanāhapariyuṭṭhānaṁ kho pana tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye parihānametaṁ.
root_text.an10.84:7.3: Makkhapariyuṭṭhānaṁ kho pana tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye parihānametaṁ.
root_text.an10.84:8.3: Paḷāsapariyuṭṭhānaṁ kho pana tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye parihānametaṁ.
root_text.an10.84:9.3: Issāpariyuṭṭhānaṁ kho pana tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye parihānametaṁ.
root_text.an10.84:10.3: Maccherapariyuṭṭhānaṁ kho pana tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye parihānametaṁ.
root_text.an10.84:11.3: Sāṭheyyapariyuṭṭhānaṁ kho pana tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye parihānametaṁ.
root_text.an10.84:12.3: Māyāpariyuṭṭhānaṁ kho pana tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye parihānametaṁ.
root_text.an10.84:13.3: Icchāpariyuṭṭhānaṁ kho pana tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye parihānametaṁ.
root_text.an10.84:14.1: Sati kho pana ayamāyasmā uttari karaṇīye oramattakena visesādhigamena antarā vosānaṁ āpanno.
root_text.an10.84:14.2: Antarā vosānagamanaṁ kho pana tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye parihānametaṁ’.
root_text.an10.84:15.1: So vatāvuso, bhikkhu ‘ime dasa dhamme appahāya imasmiṁ dhammavinaye vuddhiṁ virūḷhiṁ vepullaṁ āpajjissatī’ti netaṁ ṭhānaṁ vijjati.
root_text.an10.84:15.2: So vatāvuso, bhikkhu ‘ime dasa dhamme pahāya imasmiṁ dhammavinaye vuddhiṁ virūḷhiṁ vepullaṁ āpajjissatī’ti ṭhānametaṁ vijjatī”ti.

translation_text.an10.84:1.1: There Venerable Mahāmoggallāna addressed the mendicants:
translation_text.an10.84:2.2: ‘I understand: “Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.”’
translation_text.an10.84:2.3: They’re pursued, pressed, and grilled by the Realized One, or by one of his disciples who is a meditator skilled in attainments, in the minds of others, and in encompassing another’s mind.
translation_text.an10.84:2.4: Grilled in this way they get stuck or lose their way. They fall to ruin and disaster.
translation_text.an10.84:3.1: The Realized One or one of his disciples encompasss their mind and investigates:
translation_text.an10.84:3.2: ‘Why does this venerable declare enlightenment, saying:
translation_text.an10.84:3.3: “I understand: ‘Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.’?”’
translation_text.an10.84:5.2: and often lives with a heart full of anger.
translation_text.an10.84:5.3: But being full of anger means decline in the teaching and training proclaimed by the Realized One.
translation_text.an10.84:13.2: and often lives with a heart full of desire.
translation_text.an10.84:13.3: But being full of desire means decline in the teaching and training proclaimed by the Realized One.
translation_text.an10.84:14.1: When there is still more to be done, this venerable stopped half-way after achieving some trifling distinction.
translation_text.an10.84:14.2: But stopping half-way means decline in the teaching and training proclaimed by the Realized One.’
translation_text.an10.84:15.1: It is quite impossible for a mendicant to achieve growth, improvement, or maturity in this teaching and training without giving up these ten qualities.
translation_text.an10.84:15.2: It is quite possible for a mendicant to achieve growth, improvement, or maturity in this teaching and training after giving up these ten qualities.”
